variants.name: Row (default)
variants.note: Use EuiFlexGroup for a single-row horizontal layout — pair with EuiFlexItem children and gutterSize to control spacing.

variants.name: Column
variants.note: Use EuiFlexGroup with direction="column" to stack items vertically instead of horizontally.

variants.name: Wrap
variants.note: Use EuiFlexGroup with wrap={true} so items wrap onto the next row when they exceed the container width.

variants.name: Responsive
variants.note: Use EuiFlexGroup with responsive={false} to prevent automatic stacking on mobile breakpoints.

keyProps.name: grow
keyProps.desc: false to prevent growing, number for ratio

EuiHeader.import: import { EuiHeader, EuiHeaderSection, EuiHeaderSectionItem, EuiHeaderLogo } from '@elastic/eui';

variants.name: Fixed
variants.desc: position='fixed' sticks to top of viewport
variants.note: Use EuiHeader with position="fixed" to stick the header to the top of the viewport — combine with EuiHeaderSection and EuiHeaderSectionItem for layout.

variants.name: Dark
variants.note: Use EuiHeader with theme="dark" for an inverted dark color scheme, typically used for the top-level app navigation bar.

variants.name: Stacked
variants.desc: Multiple EuiHeaders stacked for app + section nav
variants.note: Use multiple EuiHeader components stacked vertically — the first for global app nav, the second for section-level navigation.

variants.name: Title only
variants.note: Use EuiPageHeader with just pageTitle and an optional description string for a simple section heading.

variants.name: With actions
variants.note: Use EuiPageHeader with rightSideItems as an array of buttons or controls placed on the right side of the header.

variants.name: With tabs
variants.desc: tabs prop renders navigation below the title
variants.note: Use EuiPageHeader with the tabs prop to render a tab bar directly below the page title for section navigation.

variants.name: With breadcrumbs
variants.note: Use EuiPageHeader with breadcrumbs prop to render a breadcrumb trail above the page title.

variants.name: With icon or avatar
variants.note: Use EuiPageHeader with iconType to display an icon or logo to the left of the page title.

variants.name: Plain with shadow
variants.note: Use EuiPanel with hasShadow={true} for a standard elevated surface.

variants.name: Bordered
variants.note: Use EuiPanel with hasBorder={true} and hasShadow={false} for a flat bordered card without elevation.

variants.name: Subdued
variants.note: Use EuiPanel with color="subdued" for a gray background section that visually separates content.

variants.name: Status color
variants.desc: semantic color for alerts or highlights
variants.note: Use EuiPanel with color="primary", "success", "warning", or "danger" for semantic status highlights.

variants.name: Clickable (card)
variants.note: Use EuiPanel with onClick for a clickable card — add cursor: pointer via style.

EuiFlyout.import: import { EuiFlyout, EuiFlyoutHeader, EuiFlyoutBody, EuiFlyoutFooter } from '@elastic/eui';

variants.name: Overlay (default)
variants.desc: covers page with mask, traps focus
variants.note: Use the default overlay variant of EuiFlyout — it covers the page with a mask and traps focus inside the panel.

variants.name: Push
variants.desc: page stays interactive, adds body padding
variants.note: Use the Push variant of EuiFlyout — type="push" keeps the page interactive by adding body padding instead of an overlay mask.

variants.name: Resizable
variants.note: Use the Resizable variant of EuiFlyout — add resizable={true} with minWidth and maxWidth so users can drag to resize the panel.

variants.name: Without ownFocus
variants.note: Use EuiFlyout without ownFocus — set ownFocus={false} so the page content remains visible and clickable behind the flyout.

variants.name: Child flyout (session)
variants.desc: side-by-side child panel with history nav
variants.note: Use the Child flyout session variant of EuiFlyout — set session="start" on the parent and session="inherit" on the child to render side-by-side panels with built-in history navigation.

variants.name: With banner
variants.desc: EuiCallOut banner at top of body
variants.note: Use EuiFlyout with a banner — pass an EuiCallOut to the banner prop of EuiFlyoutBody to display a pinned notice at the top of the flyout body.

EuiModal.import: import { EuiModal, EuiModalHeader, EuiModalHeaderTitle, EuiModalBody, EuiModalFooter } from '@elastic/eui';

variants.name: Default
variants.desc: full modal with header, body, footer
variants.note: Use EuiModal with EuiModalHeader, EuiModalBody, and EuiModalFooter for a standard dialog — always include aria-labelledby pointing to the title id.

variants.name: Confirm dialog
variants.note: Use EuiConfirmModal for yes/no destructive confirmations — set buttonColor="danger" and defaultFocusedButton="confirm".

variants.name: Form modal
variants.note: Use EuiModal containing an EuiForm when you need a dialog with form inputs — wire the submit button to your form handler.

variants.name: Default
variants.desc: Basic collapsible section with arrow toggle
variants.note: Use EuiAccordion for a basic collapsible section with an arrow toggle — always provide a unique id prop for accessibility.

variants.name: With button content
variants.desc: buttonContent prop for custom trigger label
variants.note: Use EuiAccordion with buttonContent prop to fully customize the trigger label with icons or rich content.

variants.name: Extra actions
variants.desc: extraAction prop adds element on the right of the trigger
variants.note: Use EuiAccordion with extraAction prop to add an icon button or action on the right side of the trigger row.

variants.name: Bordered
variants.desc: borders and padding for card-like sections
variants.note: Use EuiAccordion with borders and paddingSize for a card-like collapsible section with visual separation.

variants.name: Flush
variants.note: Use EuiAccordion with paddingSize="none" for tight layouts where you control your own internal padding.

variants.name: Force open/closed
variants.note: Use EuiAccordion with forceState="open" or "closed" to control the expanded state from outside the component.

variants.name: Default
variants.desc: Anchored panel with arrow pointing to trigger
variants.note: Use EuiPopover as an anchored floating panel — pass the trigger as the button prop and control visibility with isOpen and closePopover.

variants.name: Panel-less
variants.note: Use EuiPopover with hasPanel={false} to render custom floating content without the default panel background.

variants.name: With title
variants.note: Use EuiPopover with EuiPopoverTitle inside to pin a bold title at the top of the popover panel.

variants.name: With footer
variants.note: Use EuiPopover with EuiPopoverFooter inside to pin action buttons at the bottom of the popover panel.

variants.name: Input popover
variants.desc: EuiInputPopover anchors to a form input
variants.note: Use EuiInputPopover to anchor a floating panel directly below a form input — useful for custom autocomplete or date pickers.

variants.name: Default
variants.desc: appears on hover above the element
variants.note: Use EuiToolTip wrapping any element — it shows the content prop as a tooltip on hover, positioned above by default.

variants.name: Positioned
variants.note: Use EuiToolTip with position="bottom", "left", or "right" to control which side the tooltip appears on.

variants.name: With title
variants.desc: bold heading above the tooltip body
variants.note: Use EuiToolTip with a title prop to add a bold heading above the tooltip description text.

variants.name: On disabled elements
variants.desc: wrap in a span to enable tooltip on disabled buttons
variants.note: Use EuiToolTip wrapping a span around a disabled element — disabled elements do not fire mouse events so the span is required.

variants.name: Info / primary
variants.note: Use EuiCallOut with color="primary" for informational messages that need attention without urgency.

variants.name: Success
variants.note: Use EuiCallOut with color="success" to confirm a completed action.

variants.name: Warning
variants.note: Use EuiCallOut with color="warning" to alert users to something that needs review.

variants.name: Danger / error
variants.note: Use EuiCallOut with color="danger" for errors or critical failures.

variants.name: Dismissible
variants.note: Use EuiCallOut with onDismiss to let users close the callout — manage visibility with local state.

variants.name: Default
variants.desc: Vertical layout with title, description, footer
variants.note: Use EuiCard with title, description, and optional footer for a standard vertical content card.

variants.name: Horizontal
variants.desc: layout='horizontal' for icon + text side by side
variants.note: Use EuiCard with layout="horizontal" to place an icon beside the text — good for feature lists or compact option cards.

variants.name: Selectable
variants.desc: selectable prop turns card into a checkbox
variants.note: Use EuiCard with selectable prop to turn it into a checkbox-style selection card — manages its own selected and disabled state.

variants.name: Clickable
variants.note: Use EuiCard with onClick to make the entire card a clickable navigation element.

variants.name: Beta badge
variants.desc: betaBadgeProps to show a badge on the card
variants.note: Use EuiCard with betaBadgeProps to display a beta or experimental badge in the top corner of the card.

variants.name: Primary (filled)
variants.note: Use EuiButton with fill={true} as the primary CTA — this is the solid filled style for the main action on a page.

variants.name: Secondary (empty)
variants.note: Use EuiButtonEmpty as a secondary or tertiary action — it has no background, suitable for cancel or low-emphasis actions.

variants.name: Icon button
variants.note: Use EuiButtonIcon for icon-only actions — always include an aria-label for accessibility.

variants.name: With icon
variants.note: Use EuiButton with iconType to add a leading icon alongside the button label.

variants.name: Loading state
variants.note: Use EuiButton with isLoading={true} to show a spinner during async operations.

variants.name: Danger
variants.note: Use EuiButton with color="danger" and fill={true} for destructive actions like delete or remove.

variants.name: Button group (segmented)
variants.note: Use EuiButtonGroup for segmented controls — type="single" behaves like a radio group, type="multi" like checkboxes.

variants.name: Default
variants.note: Use EuiButtonEmpty as a secondary or cancel action — it has no background fill and lower visual weight than EuiButton.

variants.name: With icon
variants.note: Use EuiButtonEmpty with iconType to add a leading icon alongside the label for contextual actions like add, edit, or external link.

variants.name: Flush
variants.desc: flush removes padding on one side for edge alignment
variants.note: Use EuiButtonEmpty with flush="left" or flush="right" to remove padding on that side so it aligns flush with surrounding content.

variants.name: Danger
variants.note: Use EuiButtonEmpty with color="danger" for a low-emphasis destructive action like remove or disconnect.

variants.name: Extra small
variants.note: Use EuiButtonEmpty with size="xs" for compact toolbar or inline actions where space is constrained.

variants.name: Default
variants.note: Use EuiButtonIcon for icon-only actions — always provide aria-label for accessibility since there is no visible text.

variants.name: Filled
variants.note: Use EuiButtonIcon with display="fill" for a solid background icon button that needs more visual prominence.

variants.name: Base
variants.note: Use EuiButtonIcon with display="base" for a subtle bordered background, useful in toolbars and action columns.

variants.name: Danger
variants.note: Use EuiButtonIcon with color="danger" for destructive actions like delete or remove in table row actions.

variants.name: In table actions
variants.note: Use EuiButtonIcon in EuiBasicTable actions column — combine edit (pencil icon) and delete (trash icon, danger color) as the standard row action pattern.

variants.name: Single select
variants.note: Use EuiButtonGroup with type="single" for a segmented control that behaves like a radio group — one selection at a time.

variants.name: Multi select
variants.note: Use EuiButtonGroup with type="multi" for a toggle group where multiple buttons can be active simultaneously.

variants.name: Icon only
variants.note: Use EuiButtonGroup with isIconOnly={true} to show icon-only buttons — each button automatically gets a tooltip from its label.

variants.name: Default
variants.note: Use EuiBreadcrumbs with a breadcrumbs array of { text, href } objects for a standard navigation trail.

variants.name: Truncated
variants.desc: max={n} collapses middle items into a popover
variants.note: Use EuiBreadcrumbs with max={n} to collapse middle items into a popover when the trail is long.

variants.name: Application context
variants.note: Use EuiBreadcrumbs with type="application" for Kibana-style application context breadcrumbs in the header.

variants.name: Default
variants.note: Use EuiTabs with EuiTab children for a standard horizontal tab bar — manage active state with isSelected on each tab.

variants.name: With bottom border
variants.note: Use EuiTabs with bottomBorder={true} for page-level tabs that sit above the main content area with a separating line.

variants.name: Small
variants.note: Use EuiTabs with size="s" for compact tabs in panels, sidebars, or flyouts.

variants.name: Scrollable
variants.desc: Overflow scrolls when tabs exceed width
variants.note: Use EuiTabs in a container narrower than the total tab width — overflow scrolls automatically on smaller screens.

variants.name: Default vertical
variants.note: Use EuiSteps with a steps array for a vertical numbered step-by-step guide — each step has a title, children, and optional status.

variants.name: With status
variants.note: Use EuiSteps with status on each step to reflect progress — values are "complete", "warning", "danger", "loading", or "incomplete".

variants.name: Horizontal progress
variants.note: Use EuiStepsHorizontal for a top-of-page progress bar style stepper — each step has a status and an onClick handler.

variants.name: Default tree
variants.note: Use EuiSideNav with a nested items tree for a collapsible sidebar navigation — mark the active item with isSelected.

variants.name: Mobile
variants.note: Use EuiSideNav with mobileTitle and toggleOpenOnMobile to support a hamburger-style toggle on mobile viewports.

variants.name: With filters
variants.desc: filters prop adds dropdown filter buttons
variants.note: Use EuiSearchBar with a filters array to add dropdown filter buttons alongside the search input.

variants.name: With schema
variants.desc: schema prop for field-aware query syntax
variants.note: Use EuiSearchBar with a schema to enable field-aware structured query syntax like status:active.

variants.name: Controlled
variants.desc: query prop + onChange for controlled mode
variants.note: Use EuiSearchBar with query prop and onChange for fully controlled mode where you manage the query string externally.

variants.name: Flat
variants.note: Use EuiContextMenu with a flat single panel for a simple dropdown list of actions.

variants.name: Nested
variants.desc: panels prop with multiple levels + back nav
variants.note: Use EuiContextMenu with multiple panels and nested item references to create a drill-down menu with automatic back navigation.

keyProps.name: onClick
keyProps.desc: renders as <button> when used without href

variants.name: Anchor link
variants.note: Use EuiLink with href for a standard anchor tag — it inherits text color from context and adds hover underline.

variants.name: External link
variants.note: Use EuiLink with external={true} to add an external link icon and automatically set rel="noopener noreferrer".

variants.name: Button link
variants.note: Use EuiLink with onClick and no href to render a button styled as a link — correct semantic HTML for in-page actions.

variants.name: Colored
variants.desc: color prop for semantic or subdued links
variants.note: Use EuiLink with color="subdued" for secondary links, "danger" for destructive links, or "ghost" on dark backgrounds.

variants.name: Status color
variants.note: Use EuiBadge with color="success", "warning", "danger", or "primary" for semantic status labels.

variants.name: Custom color
variants.note: Use EuiBadge with a hex color string for custom brand or category color coding.

variants.name: Clickable
variants.desc: onClick turns badge into a button
variants.note: Use EuiBadge with onClick to make it an interactive filter or toggle element.

variants.name: With icon
variants.note: Use EuiBadge with iconType and iconSide to add a leading or trailing icon to the badge label.

variants.name: Notification badge
variants.note: Use EuiNotificationBadge to display a count indicator, typically overlaid on a button or avatar.

variants.name: With icon
variants.note: Use EuiEmptyPrompt with an icon and actions for a standard empty state — wrap the title in an h2.

variants.name: Loading
variants.note: Use EuiEmptyPrompt with EuiLoadingLogo as the icon prop for an async loading state.

variants.name: Error
variants.note: Use EuiEmptyPrompt with color="danger" and iconType="warning" for an error or failed state.

variants.name: No permissions
variants.note: Use EuiEmptyPrompt with color="subdued" and iconType="lock" for a no-permissions or access-restricted state.

variants.name: First use / onboarding
variants.note: Use EuiEmptyPrompt as a first-use onboarding state — include a primary CTA button in the actions prop to guide users to the next step.

variants.name: Default body
variants.note: Use EuiText to wrap body content — it applies consistent EUI typography to any HTML elements like p, ul, ol, or h tags inside it.

variants.name: Small
variants.desc: size=s for secondary or supporting text
variants.note: Use EuiText with size="s" for secondary descriptions, help text, or metadata below a primary element.

variants.name: Extra small
variants.note: Use EuiText with size="xs" for captions, timestamps, or fine-print labels where minimal font size is needed.

variants.name: Subdued
variants.note: Use EuiText with color="subdued" to visually de-emphasise supporting or secondary content.

variants.name: Ghost
variants.desc: color=ghost for text on dark backgrounds
variants.note: Use EuiText with color="ghost" for white text on dark or colored panel backgrounds.

variants.name: Large (l/m)
variants.note: Use EuiTitle with size="l" or "m" for page-level headings — wrap an h1 or h2 inside for correct semantic HTML.

variants.name: Small (s/xs)
variants.note: Use EuiTitle with size="s" or "xs" for section headings inside panels, flyouts, or cards.

variants.name: Extra small (xxs/xxxs)
variants.note: Use EuiTitle with size="xxs" or "xxxs" for compact UI labels, group headings, or tight sidebar sections.

variants.name: Uppercase
variants.note: Use EuiTitle with textTransform="uppercase" for category labels or section dividers that follow an all-caps convention.

variants.name: Inline
variants.desc: EuiCode wraps short inline snippets in <code>
variants.note: Use EuiCode for short inline code snippets within a sentence or paragraph.

variants.name: Block
variants.note: Use EuiCodeBlock for multiline code with syntax highlighting — add isCopyable={true} for a copy button and overflowHeight to cap the height.

variants.name: Default
variants.desc: Large number with small label above
variants.note: Use EuiStat with a large title value and a description label for KPI metrics and summary numbers.

variants.name: Reversed
variants.note: Use EuiStat with the title above and description below by reversing the titleElement order.

variants.name: With color
variants.note: Use EuiStat with titleColor="success", "danger", or "warning" to add semantic meaning to the metric value.

variants.name: Loading
variants.note: Use EuiStat with isLoading={true} to show a skeleton placeholder while the value is being fetched.

variants.name: Success
variants.desc: green dot for healthy or active state
variants.note: Use EuiHealth with color="success" to indicate a healthy, active, or running status.

variants.name: Warning
variants.desc: yellow dot for degraded or at-risk state
variants.note: Use EuiHealth with color="warning" to indicate a degraded, at-risk, or needs-attention status.

variants.name: Danger
variants.desc: red dot for failed or critical state
variants.note: Use EuiHealth with color="danger" to indicate a failed, critical, or offline status.

variants.name: Subdued
variants.desc: gray dot for inactive or unknown state
variants.note: Use EuiHealth with color="subdued" for an inactive, paused, or unknown status where no semantic color applies.

variants.name: Custom color
variants.desc: hex string for non-standard status colors
variants.note: Use EuiHealth with a hex color string when you need a custom status color outside the standard semantic palette.

variants.name: Initials
variants.desc: Derives initials from name prop automatically
variants.note: Use EuiAvatar with a name prop — it automatically derives initials and picks a background color.

variants.name: Image
variants.note: Use EuiAvatar with imageUrl to display a photo instead of initials.

variants.name: Icon
variants.desc: iconType to use an EUI icon instead
variants.note: Use EuiAvatar with iconType to show an EUI icon instead of initials — useful for system or entity avatars.

variants.name: Disabled
variants.note: Use EuiAvatar with isDisabled={true} to render a grayed-out avatar for inactive or disabled users.

keyProps.name: name
keyProps.desc: required — used for initials and aria-label

variants.name: Spinner
variants.note: Use EuiLoadingSpinner for an inline or overlay circular loading indicator — control size with the size prop.

variants.name: Elastic logo
variants.desc: EuiLoadingLogo — animated logo, use in empty states
variants.note: Use EuiLoadingLogo with logo="logoElastic" as the icon in EuiEmptyPrompt for a full-page loading state.

variants.name: Skeleton text
variants.note: Use EuiSkeletonText to show a placeholder for text content while data is loading.

variants.name: Skeleton rectangle
variants.note: Use EuiSkeletonRectangle to show a placeholder for block-level content like images or cards while loading.

variants.name: Determinate
variants.note: Use EuiProgress with value and max for a determinate progress bar that shows known completion percentage.

variants.name: Indeterminate
variants.note: Use EuiProgress without a value for an indeterminate animated loading bar when completion is unknown.

variants.name: Fixed
variants.note: Use EuiProgress with position="fixed" for a thin top-of-page loading indicator during page transitions.

variants.name: Info (default)
variants.note: Use EuiGlobalToastList with a toasts array for standard informational notifications.

variants.name: Success
variants.note: Use EuiGlobalToastList with color="success" on a toast for positive confirmation messages.

variants.name: Warning
variants.note: Use EuiGlobalToastList with color="warning" on a toast for non-critical alerts that need attention.

variants.name: Danger
variants.note: Use EuiGlobalToastList with color="danger" on a toast for error notifications.

variants.name: Default
variants.desc: Plain list of text items or links
variants.note: Use EuiListGroup with listItems for a plain list of text items or navigation links.

variants.name: Flush
variants.note: Use EuiListGroup with flush={true} to remove the outer border and padding for a seamless inline list.

variants.name: Bordered
variants.desc: bordered={true} adds a border around the group
variants.note: Use EuiListGroup with bordered={true} to wrap the list in a visible border for visual grouping.

variants.name: With icons
variants.desc: EuiListGroupItem iconType for icons on each item
variants.note: Use EuiListGroup with EuiListGroupItem iconType to add a leading icon to each item.

variants.name: With actions
variants.desc: EuiListGroupItem extraAction for icon button on hover
variants.note: Use EuiListGroup with EuiListGroupItem extraAction to show an icon button on hover for per-item actions.

variants.name: Column
variants.note: Use EuiDescriptionList with type="column" to lay out key-value pairs side by side in two columns.

variants.name: Inline
variants.desc: type='inline' label and value on same line
variants.note: Use EuiDescriptionList with type="inline" to display label and value on the same line, separated by a colon.

variants.name: Row (default)
variants.note: Use EuiDescriptionList with the default row type to stack the label above its value for metadata displays.

variants.name: Responsive column
variants.note: Use EuiDescriptionList with type="responsiveColumn" to show columns on desktop and switch to stacked rows on mobile.

variants.name: With form row
variants.note: Use EuiFieldText inside EuiFormRow to get a labelled, accessible text input with optional help text.

variants.name: Validation error
variants.note: Use EuiFieldText with isInvalid={true} inside EuiFormRow with error prop to display validation errors.

variants.name: With prepend / append
variants.desc: input group with label or button
variants.note: Use EuiFieldText with prepend and append props to create an input group with labels or action buttons.

variants.name: Compressed (dense UI)
variants.desc: shorter height for toolbar or table use
variants.note: Use EuiFieldText with compressed={true} inside EuiFormRow with display="rowCompressed" for dense toolbar or table filter UIs.

variants.name: Default
variants.desc: label above input with optional help text
variants.note: Use EuiFormRow to wrap any form control — it provides the label, help text, and error message layout automatically.

variants.name: With validation error
variants.desc: isInvalid + error shows red error message
variants.note: Use EuiFormRow with isInvalid={true} and error prop to display validation error messages below the input in red.

variants.name: Compressed
variants.note: Use EuiFormRow with display="rowCompressed" for toolbars, filter bars, or any dense UI where vertical space is limited.

variants.name: Column layout
variants.note: Use EuiFormRow with display="columnCompressed" to place the label inline to the left of the input in a horizontal layout.

variants.name: No label
variants.desc: hasEmptyLabelSpace reserves space without a label
variants.note: Use EuiFormRow with hasEmptyLabelSpace={true} to align a control with adjacent labeled inputs without showing a label.

variants.name: Multi select
variants.desc: default — select multiple options as tags
variants.note: Use EuiComboBox for multi-select — selected values appear as removable tags, options filtered by typing.

variants.name: Single select
variants.note: Use EuiComboBox with singleSelection={{ asPlainText: true }} to restrict selection to one option.

variants.name: Async / server-side
variants.note: Use EuiComboBox with onSearchChange for async server-side filtering — set isLoading={true} while fetching.

variants.name: Create custom option
variants.note: Use EuiComboBox with onCreateOption to allow users to type and create custom values not in the options list.

variants.name: Default
variants.note: Use EuiSelect for a native browser select dropdown — simple and accessible, best for short option lists.

variants.name: With empty option
variants.note: Use EuiSelect with hasNoInitialSelection={true} to start with a blank placeholder option before any selection is made.

variants.name: Compressed
variants.note: Use EuiSelect with compressed={true} for a shorter height select in toolbars or filter bars.

variants.name: Disabled
variants.note: Use EuiSelect with disabled={true} when the field should be visible but not editable in the current context.

variants.name: Default
variants.desc: Custom dropdown with rich option content
variants.note: Use EuiSuperSelect when options need rich content beyond a plain text label — use inputDisplay for the trigger and dropdownDisplay for the option.

variants.name: With descriptions
variants.desc: inputDisplay vs dropdownDisplay for trigger vs option
variants.note: Use EuiSuperSelect with inputDisplay and dropdownDisplay to show a short label in the trigger but a full description in the dropdown.

variants.name: Full width
variants.note: Use EuiSuperSelect with fullWidth={true} to stretch the select to fill its container.

variants.name: Default
variants.note: Use EuiSwitch with a label, checked, and onChange — the label is always required for accessibility even if visually hidden.

variants.name: Compressed
variants.note: Use EuiSwitch with compressed={true} for a smaller toggle in toolbars, settings rows, or table cells.

variants.name: Hidden label
variants.note: Use EuiSwitch with showLabel={false} to hide the label visually while keeping it accessible to screen readers.

variants.name: Loading
variants.note: Use EuiSwitch with isLoading={true} to show a loading state while an async toggle operation is in progress.

variants.name: Single
variants.desc: EuiCheckbox for a single controlled checkbox
variants.note: Use EuiCheckbox for a single controlled checkbox — always provide a unique id for the label association.

variants.name: Group
variants.note: Use EuiCheckboxGroup for a set of related checkboxes — pass an options array and manage idToSelectedMap.

variants.name: Indeterminate
variants.note: Use EuiCheckbox with indeterminate={true} for a parent checkbox that represents a partially selected group.

variants.name: Default vertical
variants.note: Use EuiRadioGroup with an options array and idSelected for a standard vertical stack of mutually exclusive radio options.

variants.name: Compressed
variants.note: Use EuiRadioGroup with compressed={true} for tighter vertical spacing in settings panels or compact forms.

variants.name: With legend
variants.note: Use EuiRadioGroup with a legend prop to provide an accessible group label via fieldset/legend — important when the group context is not obvious from surrounding content.

variants.name: Single
variants.note: Use EuiRange for a single-thumb slider with min, max, and step — control the value with value and onChange.

variants.name: Dual
variants.desc: EuiDualRange — two thumbs for min/max range
variants.note: Use EuiDualRange for a two-thumb range selector that lets users pick a minimum and maximum value.

variants.name: With input
variants.note: Use EuiRange with showInput="inputWithPopover" to let users type a precise value directly into an input field.

variants.name: With ticks
variants.note: Use EuiRange with showTicks={true} to display step markers along the track for discrete value selection.

variants.name: Toggle filters
variants.note: Use EuiFilterGroup with EuiFilterButton and hasActiveFilters to show toggle-style filter buttons with active state.

variants.name: With popover
variants.note: Use EuiFilterGroup with EuiFilterButton combined with EuiPopover to open a filter options dropdown panel.

variants.name: Full width
variants.note: Use EuiFilterGroup with fullWidth={true} to stretch the filter bar to fill its container width.

variants.name: Single select
variants.desc: Clicking an item selects it and deselects others
variants.note: Use EuiSelectable for a single-select filterable list — clicking an item selects it and deselects all others.

variants.name: Multi select
variants.note: Use EuiSelectable for a multi-select list — the default mode allows any number of items to be selected.

variants.name: With search
variants.note: Use EuiSelectable with searchable={true} to add a search input above the list for filtering options by typing.

variants.name: With custom render
variants.note: Use EuiSelectable with renderOption to fully customize how each list item is displayed.

variants.name: Virtualized
variants.desc: Handles thousands of items via windowing
variants.note: Use EuiSelectable for large datasets — it uses windowing internally to handle thousands of items without performance issues.

variants.name: Default
variants.note: Use EuiBasicTable with items and columns for a simple data table — add an actions column for row-level edit and delete buttons.

variants.name: With pagination and sorting
variants.note: Use EuiBasicTable with pagination and sorting props and an onChange handler to manage server-side data fetching.

variants.name: With row selection
variants.note: Use EuiBasicTable with itemId and selection props to enable checkbox row selection.

variants.name: Default
variants.desc: Auto-handles sort, paginate, search from items array
variants.note: Use EuiInMemoryTable when all data is available client-side — it handles sorting, pagination, and search automatically from the items array.

variants.name: With search
variants.desc: search prop adds EuiSearchBar above table
variants.note: Use EuiInMemoryTable with search={true} or a search config to add a built-in EuiSearchBar above the table.

variants.name: With selection
variants.note: Use EuiInMemoryTable with selection prop to enable checkbox row selection managed automatically.

variants.name: Default
variants.note: Use EuiDataGrid for large virtualized datasets — provide rowCount and a renderCellValue function instead of pre-rendered rows.

variants.name: With toolbar
variants.note: Use EuiDataGrid with toolbarVisibility to control which toolbar controls are shown — column visibility, sort, density, and fullscreen.

variants.name: Cell popovers
variants.note: Use EuiDataGrid with renderCellPopover to show expanded cell content in a popover when a cell is clicked.

variants.name: Row heights
variants.desc: rowHeightsOptions for auto or fixed height rows
variants.note: Use EuiDataGrid with rowHeightsOptions to set auto-sizing or fixed pixel heights per row.

variants.name: Default
variants.desc: Full-page layout with sidebar and content area
variants.note: Use EuiPageTemplate as the root layout wrapper for a full Kibana-style page with consistent padding and max-width.

variants.name: With sidebar
variants.note: Use EuiPageTemplate.Sidebar for a persistent left-side panel — combine with EuiSideNav for section navigation.

variants.name: Empty state
variants.note: Use EuiPageTemplate.EmptyPrompt to center an EuiEmptyPrompt within the page layout for zero-state or error pages.

variants.name: Bottom bar
variants.note: Use EuiPageTemplate.BottomBar for a fixed footer that holds save or action buttons pinned to the bottom of the viewport.

variants.name: Centered body
variants.note: Use EuiPageTemplate with panelled={false} for a centered content layout without the panel background.

variants.name: Default
variants.desc: Editor with preview toggle and toolbar
variants.note: Use EuiMarkdownEditor for a full write-and-preview markdown editor with toolbar — bind value and onChange for controlled mode.

variants.name: Read-only preview
variants.note: Use EuiMarkdownFormat to render markdown as formatted HTML without any editing capability.

variants.name: Custom plugins
variants.note: Use EuiMarkdownEditor with parsingPlugins and processingPlugins to extend the editor with custom syntax and rendering.

variants.name: Horizontal
variants.note: Use EuiResizableContainer with direction="horizontal" to create two side-by-side panels with a draggable divider.

variants.name: Vertical
variants.note: Use EuiResizableContainer with direction="vertical" to create two stacked panels with a draggable divider.

variants.name: With collapse
variants.desc: EuiResizablePanel mode='collapsible' to collapse a panel
variants.note: Use EuiResizableContainer with EuiResizablePanel mode="collapsible" to allow one panel to be fully collapsed.

variants.name: Default
variants.desc: Swatch + hex input that opens color picker popover
variants.note: Use EuiColorPicker as a swatch-and-input that opens a full color picker popover — bind color and onChange.

variants.name: Swatches only
variants.note: Use EuiColorPicker with mode="swatch" to restrict selection to a predefined palette of swatches only.

variants.name: Inline
variants.note: Use EuiColorPicker with display="inline" to always show the color picker without a popover trigger.

variants.name: Single date
variants.note: Use EuiDatePicker for a single date — bind selected to a moment object and handle onChange to update it.

variants.name: Date range
variants.note: Use EuiDatePickerRange with startDateControl and endDateControl to let users select a date range.

variants.name: With time
variants.desc: showTimeSelect to pick time alongside date
variants.note: Use EuiDatePicker with showTimeSelect={true} to add a time picker alongside the calendar.
